purpose.flag:runtime_cleanup_enabled: Housekeeping switch — lets the server quietly tidy up stale runtime data so the world stays tuned.
purpose.flag:mainship_send_enabled: Turns on sending your flagship out on its own — the first step of flying your command ship into the world.
purpose.flag:mainship_space_movement_enabled: Lets your flagship actually move through open space rather than just hop between ports.
purpose.flag:mainship_coordinate_travel_enabled: Lets you order your flagship to travel to an exact spot on the map by coordinates.
purpose.flag:mainship_additional_commission_enab: Unlocks extra commissions for your flagship — more jobs it can take on.
purpose.flag:trade_market_enabled: The master switch for buying and selling goods at port markets — turns the whole trade economy on.
purpose.flag:trade_relief_enabled: Turns on price relief so a port that's been drained doesn't stay broken — keeps trade healthy.
purpose.flag:exploration_enabled: Turns on exploration — lets you scout and reveal new zones of the world.
purpose.flag:mining_enabled: Turns on mining — lets your ships pull ore out of asteroid belts.
purpose.flag:module_crafting_enabled: Turns on crafting ship modules — building your own weapons and gear from materials.
purpose.flag:module_fitting_enabled: Turns on fitting modules to ships — actually installing the gear you've built.
purpose.flag:captain_assignment_enabled: Turns on assigning captains to ships — putting an officer in command of a hull.
purpose.flag:captain_progression_enabled: Turns on captain growth — letting your officers earn experience and get better.
purpose.flag:ranking_enabled: Turns on the competitive leaderboards and ranking seasons.
purpose.flag:location_investment_enabled: Turns on investing in ports — sinking credits into a place to develop it over time.
purpose.flag:world_balance_enabled: Turns on the world's self-balancing — prices drift and fields deplete so the economy stays alive.
purpose.flag:phase20_polish_enabled: A polish switch for a round of quality-of-life improvements across the game.
purpose.flag:station_storage_enabled: Turns on stashing goods at a port — a storage locker so you don't have to carry everything.
purpose.flag:team_command_enabled: Turns on commanding a team of ships together (the earlier form of fleet command).
purpose.flag:salvage_market_enabled: Turns on the salvage market — selling the wreckage and loot from battles for credits.
purpose.flag:haul_contracts_enabled: Turns on haul contracts — paid delivery jobs carrying cargo between ports.
purpose.flag:captain_growth_enabled: Turns on captains earning XP and leveling up as they serve.
purpose.flag:shipyard_enabled: Turns on the shipyard — building brand-new ships instead of just buying them.
purpose.flag:ship_traits_enabled: Turns on ship traits — the unique strengths and quirks that make each hull different.
purpose.flag:launch_from_dock_enabled: Turns on launching straight from the dock you're parked at — the NO-HOME rule where ports are your only base.
purpose.flag:repair_economy_enabled: Turns on paying for repairs — the repair yards that patch up your battle-damaged ships.
purpose.flag:fleet_control_enabled: Turns on true fleet control — commanding whole fleets of ships as one.
purpose.flag:command_buffs_enabled: Turns on command bonuses — a fleet's flagship boosting the ships that sail with it.
purpose.flag:fleet_movement_unified_enabled: Turns on fleets moving as one unit instead of ship-by-ship — the whole formation travels together.
purpose.flag:timed_docking_enabled: Turns on docking taking real time — arriving at a port is a journey you can watch, not instant.
purpose.flag:per_ship_targeting_enabled: Turns on aiming each ship at its own target in a fight, instead of the whole fleet firing as one.
purpose.flag:module_range_attributes_enabled: Turns on weapon range mattering — modules now reach only so far, so positioning counts.
purpose.flag:combat_telegraph_enabled: Turns on combat telegraphs — warning cues that show an attack coming before it lands.
purpose.system:Auth: Sign-in plumbing so the game knows who you are and keeps your fleet, your money, and your progress yours alone.
purpose.system:Map: The star map itself — the ports, zones, and coordinates that make up the world you sail your ships across.
purpose.system:Reference/Config: The game's rulebook and tuning knobs — the fixed numbers and on/off switches that decide how everything behaves.
purpose.system:World State: The shared living universe — the single source of truth for what is happening in the world right now.
purpose.system:Base: Docking at a port. There is no home base — every port you dock at becomes the place your ships act from.
purpose.system:Fleet: Your fleet — the groups of ships you command and send out together as one force.
purpose.system:Movement: How your ships and fleets travel — sailing across the map from one point to another and arriving where you sent them.
purpose.system:Presence: Who and what is where right now — the live positions that let ships see and reach each other.
purpose.system:Combat: The heart of battle — where your ships trade fire with enemies and someone comes out on top.
purpose.system:Reward: The payoff — the credits, loot, and spoils you earn from winning fights and finishing jobs.
purpose.system:Report: The after-action log — a record of what happened in each battle so you can see who did what.
purpose.system:Production: The workshop — turning raw materials into finished parts and goods for your fleet.
purpose.system:Inventory: Your cargo holds and stores — everything you're carrying and everything you've stashed away.
purpose.system:Main Ship: Your flagship — the command ship you personally captain and send out on orders.
purpose.system:Trade Cargo: The trade goods your ships haul between ports to buy low and sell high.
purpose.system:Wallet: Your money — the credits you earn and spend on everything you build, buy, and repair.
purpose.system:Trade Market: The marketplace at each port where you buy and sell goods, with prices that shift as you trade.
purpose.system:Salvage Market: Where the wreckage and loot you drag home from battle turns back into cold hard credits.
purpose.system:Haul Contracts: Delivery jobs — haul cargo from one port to another on contract and get paid on arrival.
purpose.system:Repair Economy: The repair yards — pay to patch up battle-damaged ships and get them back into the fight.
purpose.system:Exploration: Venturing into the unknown — scouting new zones and revealing more of the world to reach.
purpose.system:Mining: Pulling ore out of asteroid belts — the raw material that funds everything you build.
purpose.system:Captain: The officers who command your ships — hire them, assign them, and grow their skills over time.
purpose.system:Modules: Ship gear — the weapons, armor, and equipment you craft to bolt onto your hulls.
purpose.system:Fitting: The outfitting bay — where you slot modules onto a ship to decide what it can do in a fight.
purpose.system:Ranking: The leaderboards — competitive seasons that rank you against every other captain in the world.
purpose.system:Location Investment: Pour credits into a port to develop it — a long-term stake that pays back as the place grows.
purpose.system:World Events: The world acting on its own — live events that stir up the map and change what's worth doing.
purpose.system:Ship Soul: What makes each ship one of a kind — the original traits and personality baked into a hull the day it's born.
purpose.phase:FLEET: The reshape that turned loose 'teams' of ships into real fleets you command as one force.
purpose.phase:FLEET-2: The finale of the fleet reshape — a flagship's command buffs that strengthen the ships sailing with it.
purpose.phase:MOVEMENT: The change where whole fleets travel together as a formation, instead of every ship moving on its own.
purpose.phase:BERTH: The foundation of the dock model — a ship is either out with a fleet or berthed at a port, never both.
purpose.phase:TERRITORY: Gives every port a territory — the area of space around it that counts as its own turf.
purpose.phase:POSLEAF: Tidies up how a ship's position and territory are tracked into one clean source on the server.
purpose.phase:TIMEDOCK: Makes docking take real time and shows the trip on the map, so arriving somewhere is a journey.
purpose.phase:MAPUX: Pulls the scattered map controls into one clean fleet-command panel.
purpose.phase:FITTING: Reworks the ship screen into a proper fitting bay where you outfit hulls with modules.
purpose.phase:RETIRE: Cleanup arc — retiring the old movement code once the new fleet-movement system proved itself.
purpose.phase:P0: The NO-HOME law made real — you launch from whatever dock you're parked at and return to a port, with no home base anywhere.
purpose.phase:P1: Gives the three starting ports their own identities, so each is worth trading at for different reasons.
purpose.phase:P2: Haul contracts and logistics — paid jobs moving cargo between ports.
purpose.phase:P3: Feeds combat loot back into the port economy — turn your salvage into something ports want to buy.
purpose.phase:P4: More of the world to explore — a new zone, difficulty tiers, and a steady drip of fresh discoveries.
purpose.phase:P5: Captain progression — officers who earn experience and grow stronger the more they serve.
purpose.phase:P6: The shipyard — ships you BUILD from materials, not just buy off the rack.
purpose.phase:P7: Deeper ship modules — tiers of gear and a whole defensive line to fit.
purpose.phase:P8: World events as a living system — things that happen in the world on their own and change the map.
purpose.phase:P9: The repair economy — paying to recover and fix up ships worn down by battle.
purpose.phase:P10: The first-session experience — a guided 'first orders' onboarding so new captains know what to do.
purpose.phase:P11: The long-arc world model — a deeper, port-centric simulation of how the whole world lives and breathes.
purpose.phase:P12: Ship souls — every ship born with its own original traits, one of a kind.
purpose.phase:P13: Regenerating shields — ship shields that soak damage and recharge over time.
purpose.rung:Rung 0: Next to switch on: Captains — putting officers in command of your ships.
purpose.rung:Rung 0.5: Next to switch on: Coordinate travel — ordering ships to an exact spot on the map.
purpose.rung:Rung 1: Next to switch on: Exploration — scouting and revealing new zones.
purpose.rung:Rung 2: Next to switch on: Mining — pulling ore from asteroid belts.
purpose.rung:Rung 3: Next to switch on: the Trade market — buying and selling goods at ports.
purpose.rung:Rung 4: Next to switch on: Station storage — stashing goods at a port instead of carrying it all.
purpose.rung:Rung 5: Next to switch on: Ranking — the competitive leaderboard seasons.
purpose.rung:Rung 6: Next to switch on: Location investment — developing ports with your credits.
purpose.rung:Rung 7: Next to switch on: World balance and world events — the world that shifts and stirs on its own.
purpose.mig:20260618000231: Clears out the old one-ship-at-a-time travel wiring now that whole fleets move together. (legacy movement schema drop)
purpose.mig:20260618000232: Removes the old one-ship travel commands for good — the fleet is the only thing that moves. (legacy movement function drop)
purpose.mig:20260618000233: Marks stretches of space as pirate territory — sail through and you risk being jumped. (danger zones + intercept)
purpose.mig:20260618000234: Gives battles real space: each ship has a position, moves, and picks targets inside the fight. (spatial combat tick)
purpose.mig:20260618000235: Lets you buy starter modules at a port shop instead of only finding them. (port outfitter)
purpose.mig:20260618000236: Makes pirate ambushes land reliably when you cross their territory. (ambush tuning)
purpose.mig:20260618000237: Adds slime-grade danger zones — a gentler tier of hostile space to learn on. (slime zones)
purpose.mig:20260618000238: Adds the developer switch that shows the in-game world-editing tools. (dev zone editor flag)
purpose.mig:20260618000239: Locks down who is allowed to create or delete pirate zones. (zone grant lockdown)
purpose.mig:20260618000240: Keeps multi-pirate battles flowing — enemy waves can no longer jam on a bookkeeping clash. (combat unit uniqueness)
purpose.mig:20260618000241: Teaches the battle log that another enemy wave can be on its way. (next-wave result)
purpose.mig:20260618000242: Once a fight turns spatial it stays spatial — no snapping back mid-battle. (sticky spatial mode)
purpose.mig:20260618000243: The security backbone for editing the world from inside the game — only the owner may command it, and every command is logged. (world editor owner spine)
purpose.fn:pirate_intercept_leg_zone_hits: Works out where your route crosses pirate territory. (route-zone hits)
purpose.fn:pirate_intercept_compute_risk: Weighs how dangerous a crossing is before the dice are rolled. (intercept risk)
purpose.fn:pirate_intercept_evaluate_leg: Rolls whether pirates actually jump you on a leg of your route. (intercept roll)
purpose.fn:command_ship_group_cancel_route: Lets you call off a plotted route mid-flight. (cancel route)
purpose.fn:command_ship_group_go_route: Sends your fleet along a plotted multi-waypoint route. (route mover)
purpose.fn:process_pirate_route_legs: Advances every travelling fleet leg by leg, checking for ambushes on the way. (route processor)
purpose.fn:pirate_intercept_preview_route: Shows you the danger along a route before you commit to flying it. (route preview)
purpose.fn:get_danger_zones: Tells the map where the hostile space is so it can be drawn. (zone read)
purpose.fn:pirate_zone_create: Draws a new pirate territory onto the world — a locked-down owner tool. (zone create)
purpose.fn:pirate_zone_delete: Erases a pirate territory from the world — a locked-down owner tool. (zone delete)
purpose.fn:combat_unit_decide_move: Decides where each ship steps during a spatial battle. (combat movement AI)
purpose.fn:buy_shop_offer_at_port: Hands over your credits and puts the module in your hold — the shop's till. (shop purchase)
purpose.fn:get_port_shop: Shows what's on the shelves at this port's outfitter. (shop read)
purpose.fn:is_owner: The one question every world-editing command asks first: is this the owner? (owner check)
purpose.fn:world_editor_ping: A do-nothing test command proving the editor's guard and audit trail work end to end. (editor ping)
purpose.table:danger_zones: The shapes of hostile space drawn on the map. (danger zones)
purpose.table:pirate_intercepts: The record of every pirate ambush that has happened. (intercepts)
purpose.table:fleet_route_legs: The legs of each fleet's plotted route, step by step. (route legs)
purpose.table:port_shop_offers: What each port's outfitter has for sale. (shop offers)
purpose.table:port_shop_receipts: Proof of every shop purchase, so buying twice by accident is impossible. (shop receipts)
purpose.table:app_owners: Who is allowed to edit the world itself — the owner list. (owners)
purpose.table:world_editor_audit: A permanent log of every world-editing command ever issued. (editor audit)
purpose.flag:pirate_intercept_enabled: The switch that turns pirate territory and ambushes on. (pirate intercept)
purpose.flag:spatial_combat_enabled: The switch that gives battles real positions and movement. (spatial combat)
purpose.flag:port_shop_enabled: The switch that opens the port shops for business. (port shop)
purpose.flag:dev_zone_editor_enabled: The developer switch that reveals the in-game world-editing tools. (dev zone editor)
purpose.phase:PIRATES: Make space dangerous: pirate territory you can see, and ambushes if you cross it.
purpose.phase:COMBAT-SPATIAL: Make battles spatial: ships hold positions, move, and target inside the fight.
purpose.phase:PORTSHOP: Give ports a shop so new modules can be bought where you dock.
purpose.phase:WORLDEDIT: Let the owner shape the world from inside the game — safely, audited, owner-only.
